reviews.author: Steven Rostedt
reviews.summary: Requested a minor change to the changelog to avoid using 'next commit'.
reviews.raw_body:
On Tue,  5 May 2026 17:09:33 +0000
Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com> wrote:

> Introduce queued_spin_release() as an arch-overridable unlock primitive,
> and make queued_spin_unlock() a generic wrapper around it.
> 
> This is a preparatory refactoring for the next commit, which adds
> contended_release tracepoint instrumentation to queued_spin_unlock().

In change logs, do not use "next commit". Instead say something like:

  In preparation for adding contended_release tracepoint instrumentation to
  queued_spin_unlock(), refactor the code to allow out of line calls when
  the tracepoint is enabled.

Or something like that.

-- Steve



> 
> Rename the existing arch-specific queued_spin_unlock() overrides on
> x86 (paravirt) and MIPS to queued_spin_release().
> 
> No functional change.
> 
> Signed-off-by: Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com>
> Reviewed-by: Paul E. McKenney <paulmck@kernel.org>

2026-05-13.patch_summary: This patch introduces a new function, queued_spin_release(), which is an arch-overridable unlock primitive. It refactors the existing arch-specific queued_spin_unlock() overrides on x86 (paravirt) and MIPS to use this new function. The change is preparatory for adding contended_release tracepoint instrumentation to queued_spin_unlock().

2026-05-14.patch_summary: This patch introduces a new function, queued_spin_release(), which is an arch-overridable unlock primitive. It refactors the existing arch-specific queued_spin_unlock() overrides on x86 (paravirt) and MIPS to use this new function. The change is preparatory for adding contended_release tracepoint instrumentation to queued_spin_unlock().
